title: Permission ownership + posture config
description: Formal shape of permission-config.yaml — the project's permission mode (per COR-028). `ownership_mode` chooses how much of the adopter's harness config the realizer owns (additive default / managed opt-in); `posture` chooses the default for unmodeled requests (lenient / strict). Document-shaped schema: the file IS the resource. Companion per COR-018 / the .pkit/schemas/ area conventions.

ownership_mode.description: `additive` (default): the realizer only adds to harness config, never replaces. `managed`: the realizer owns + regenerates its settings region (COR-002 authoritative-region tier).

posture.description: `lenient` (default): the hook abstains on unmodeled requests, deferring to the harness's normal flow. `strict`: unmodeled requests are denied.

active_profile.description: The active permission profile (per ADR-005) — the name of a `permission-profile` file shipped in `.pkit/permissions/profiles/` or project-defined in `.pkit/permissions/project/profiles/`. Set by `pkit permissions profile activate`. Absent = no profile active (only manual grants + guardrails).

confinement_accommodations.description: The adopter's chosen confinement toolkits whose NARROWING allowances `setup autonomy` applies to the OS sandbox (per ADR-008). Authoritative project state — committable because narrowing-only (a narrowing allowance never lowers the boundary). Widening allowances (excludedCommands, weaker-TLS) are NEVER recorded here; they are applied only by the explicit per-invocation `sandbox exclude` gesture. Set by `pkit permissions sandbox accommodate`.
